final_response: Logged the recurring 4-day external-access provisioning lag (Feb / Mar / May 2026) in observations.md, opened a followup to decide whether to escalate or build a pre-onboarding checklist, and noted it in today's daybook.

actions.summary: Added 2026-05-17 entry describing the recurring IT credential provisioning lag pattern for external joiners (Feb / Mar / May 2026, ~4 days each).
Opened a followup to decide whether to escalate the recurring lag to IT/team lead or pre-empt it with an onboarding checklist; awaiting operator direction.
Created today's daybook entry capturing the operator's note and where it was routed.

notes: User signal 'wanted to flag it so we don't lose track' read as: record the pattern + leave a tracking handle, not act yet. Routed the descriptive recurring-pattern half to observations.md and the 'don't lose track' half to followups.md as an open decision item.
Did not write a decisions.md entry — no cross-session decision has been made yet; only a question to resolve, which lives in followups.md.

Did not create a project folder — this is a single recurring observation, not a scoped project. If it becomes an initiative (e.g. 'fix external onboarding'), promoting it to projects/external-onboarding/ would be the natural next step.
Did not create a runbook — premature; would only make sense after deciding on the pre-onboarding-checklist path.
Bare AGENTS.md gave no templates, so kept entries minimal: dated heading + short prose, no invented schema.
